title: 1x12 Bookcase with Wainscoting Back
summary: A basic 1x12 bookcase dressed up with a beadboard back and mitered footer trim — the back panel does double duty as decoration, since it shows through the open sides.
description:
The bookcase itself is the simplest part: two sides and two shelves, screwed together with the outside edges kept flush. What elevates it is the beadboard back standing in for plain plywood — since this bookcase has no face frame or door hiding the interior, the back panel is visible from the front through the whole depth of the case, so its material choice matters more here than on a closed cabinet.

The footer trim is mitered from base moulding stock or shaped with a router and an ogee bit if you want to profile it yourself — either approach works, and buying pre-shaped base moulding saves the router setup if you don't already have the right bit. Miter the footer's corners at the shortest points as noted in the cut list, which keeps the visible reveal consistent even though the pieces meet at an angle rather than square.

tools.note: Optional, for shaping your own footer profile with an ogee bit instead of buying pre-shaped base moulding.

materials.name: Pine, 1x4, 6 ft
materials.note: Or base moulding, pre-shaped, if not routing your own footer profile.

cutList.note: Mitered at the shortest points — router an ogee profile or use pre-shaped 4" base moulding.

steps.title: Attach the shelves to the sides
steps.body: Fasten the shelves to the sides, keeping the outside edges flush.

steps.title: Add the front top trim
steps.body: Add the front top trim piece, keeping outside edges flush.

steps.title: Attach the beadboard back
steps.body: Attach the beadboard panel to the back of the case — since this bookcase has no closed sides or door, the back is visible through the whole depth of the shelves, so keep it centered and square.

steps.title: Add the footer
steps.body: Attach the footer support, keeping the front edge flush. Miter the side and front footer pieces at their shortest points and attach — route an ogee profile into the top edge yourself, or use pre-shaped 4" base moulding to skip that step.

steps.title: Add the tabletop pieces
steps.body: Attach the back tabletop trim piece, then the front tabletop piece — this completes the framing.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand every surface and edge, then finish.

images.alt: 1x12 bookcase with a beadboard wainscoting back
